summary: Crisp early‑70 set with strong Good Lovin' and High Time
blurb: The February ’70 Fillmore West show delivers a bright, balanced soundboard capture that highlights Pigpen‑driven moments and tight drum work. Good Lovin' (both halves) and High Time stand out despite a few unfortunate cuts. Overall the band is locked in, with Jerry’s vocals on point and Bob’s guitar crisp.
review: On February 6, 1970 at the Fillmore West, the Dead rolled through a classic early‑70 set that blends country‑rock tunes with lingering psychedelia. The soundboard recording (gd70-02-06.sbd.bean.3913.sbeok.shnf) is surprisingly crisp for its era—vocals are spot‑on, Bob’s guitar cuts cleanly through the mix, and the dual‑drummers lock into extended grooves that lift the Pigpen‑filled moments like Turn On Your Lovelight. Highlights include both takes of Good Lovin' (the segued pair) and a soaring High Time, while The Other One is mentioned as thunderous even though it’s omitted from this particular setlist. The only downside are several misplaced cuts—Dancin’ In The Street, Cryptical Envelopment, and High Time suffer brief drop‑outs—making the download imperfect but still a must‑listen for fans of the band’s early live chemistry.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The recording analysis labels the overall sentiment as mixed, with praise for certain songs and performance aspects but also notes about cuts; no consistent strong positive or negative trend emerges, so a neutral adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard source with crisp, balanced mix and clear vocals/guitar; despite a few cuts it captures the band's early‑70 energy best.

key_highlights: Thunderous The Other One (mentioned in reviews)
Strong Pigpen‑driven moments, especially Turn On Your Lovelight
Crisp, bright guitar and vocal mix that showcases early‑70 sound
A few misplaced cuts detract from an otherwise solid capture

band_performance.Jerry: Vocals are spot‑on, singing his heart out throughout the show.
band_performance.Bob: Guitar is crisp in the mix and playing great.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums sound great; percussion work praised especially during extended sequences.
band_performance.Billy: Drums sound great; percussion work praised especially during extended sequences.
